body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **unused Service Identities** across AWS, Azure / Microsoft Entra, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, and Alibaba Cloud.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment reviews identities and access paths and identifies accounts, principals, credentials, or assignments that do not meet the expected access-control state.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.
name: Unused Service Identities
remediationDescription:
## Remediation strategy
1. Confirm identity is unused with owners and logs.
2. Disable or remove permissions first.
3. Delete identity after validation window.

## GCP
~~~bash
gcloud iam service-accounts disable <sa>@<project-id>.iam.gserviceaccount.com
~~~

## Azure
~~~bash
az identity list --query "[].{name:name,resourceGroup:resourceGroup,principalId:principalId}" -o table
az role assignment list --assignee <principal-id> -o table
~~~

### References
- https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-manage
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/manage-user-assigned-managed-identities-azure-cli
